name: Meat Loaf Wellington
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Mix the meats together by hand in a large bowl.
3. Add Worcestershire sauce, eggs, cracker crumbs, ketchup, warm water, and soup mix. Mix by hand and shape into a loaf in a shallow baking dish. (For cleanup ease, use non-stick cooking spray.)
4. Drape the loaf with bacon strips.
5. Bake for 1 1/2 to 2 hours or until done. Cool for 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Separate 2 packages of crescent roll dough into 6 rectangles (2 crescents form one rectangle). Reserve the remaining 2 for decorating.
7. Overlap the triangles on a large floured surface to make a large rectangle. Gently press together the seams and perforations.
8. Place over meat loaf and mold to fit.
9. Trim off excess dough.
10. Use remaining rectangles to make a design for the top; cookie cutters may be used.
11. Brush dough with egg white and return loaf to the oven for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden.
12. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
